Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 156. (no title) (ABL 1064)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL EN]-ia ARAD-ka mIGI—aš-šur—[la-mur]

(1) [To the king], my [lord]: your servant Pan-Aššur-[lamur. Good] health to the king, [my] lord!

o 22

[lu-u] ⸢šul⸣-mu a-na LUGAL ⸢EN⸣-[ia]

o 33

[ša] LUGAL EN iš-pur-a-ni ma-a [UD-mu]

(3) [As to what] the king my lord wrote to me: "[The day] you see this [lett]er of mine, [......

o 44

[ša e-gír]-ti an-ni-tú ta-mar-[u-ni]

o 55

[x x x]-ka [x x]+⸢x⸣+[x x]

o 66

[x x x x] ku ⸢ab⸣ [x x x]

Reverse
rr destroyed

(Break)

Right
r.e. 1r.e. 1

[x x x x x]-an-ni ⸢x⸣+[x x x]

r.e. 22

[x x x] ḫa-ra-ma-ma GIŠ.GIGIR ⸢at⸣-[x x]

(r.e. 2) Later I [...] (my) chariot [......] about the food [......] I/they have left [......].

r.e. 33

[x x x] ina UGU a-kul-le-[e x]

r.e. 44

[x x x] ú-ra-mu-⸢u*⸣-[x x]

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334709/.
